mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.5, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with declining housing starts signals a rotation from residential investment to commercial activity, increasing corporate capex expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 55% of observations, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising policy rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of tightening episodes, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands aggregate demand and corporate profit margins, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant difference between high-GDPC1-growth and low-GDPC1-growth subsamples (p > 0.05), or Rank IC stays below 0.01 for 12 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When residential mortgage debt outstanding exceeds 440000 million dollars, household leverage reaches unsustainable levels, triggering consumption pullbacks and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where RSAFS crosses the 440000 threshold, or the signal's hit rate fails to exceed 55% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, elevating earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes (partial correlation < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, sustained industrial production growth eventually outpaces demand, causing inventory gluts and margin compression that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 50% of late-cycle production peaks, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% across all rolling 2-year windows.
